title: Phanera maximiliani
intro: Phanera maximiliani, also known as the Himalayan ironwood or Himalayan ironplum, is a medicinal tree species belonging to the family Moraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Phanera maximiliani include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of herbal supplements and formulations, such as capsules and extracts.
taxonomy: Phanera maximiliani is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family.
